input:
Some of them abused him. Soon there was some confusion and Abū Bakr was bound up by several people including ʿUtbah ibn Rabīʿah, who used his shoes to beat Abū Bakr’s face. When he was rescued by his own tribesmen, they thought that he was dead. Towards the evening, he came round. The first thing he did was to enquire how God’s Messenger was. He would not rest until he had been taken to the Prophet. They took him after nightfall, and he walked, supported by his mother and a Muslim woman, until he arrived at the Prophet’s home.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 8: Objective Containment

output.scene_title: Abu Bakr's Rescue and Visit to the Prophet
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 8: Objective Containment

characters.name: Abu Bakr al-Siddiq
characters.appearance: Late 40s, light olive skin, slender build, slightly shorter than average. Thin grey-streaked beard with slight reddish henna tint, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Kind gentle eyes, slightly stooped shoulders.

characters.name: ʿUtbah ibn Rabīʿah
characters.appearance: Mid 40s, medium build, rough features. Dark brown skin, scruffy beard. Wearing simple brown woolen thobe, leather belt, worn leather sandals. Expression shows aggression.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Abu Bakr's Mother (RA)
characters.appearance: Late 40s, medium build, gentle features. Light olive skin, soft grey hair tied under a white cloth head covering. Wearing a simple pale blue thobe, loose-fitting brown cloak, worn leather sandals. Expression shows concern and care.

characters.name: Anonymous Muslim Woman
characters.appearance: Early 30s, medium build, modest features. Dark brown skin, simple head covering. Wearing a simple pale brown thobe, loose-fitting beige cloak, worn leather sandals. Expression shows compassion and support.

output.moral_lesson: The importance of showing kindness and compassion to those who are hurt or in need.
output.narrative_text: Abu Bakr was badly beaten by his fellow Quraysh, but he was concerned for the Prophet's well-being. After he was rescued, he insisted on being taken to the Prophet's home, where he was supported by his mother and a Muslim woman.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, Abū Bakr shown with full face, injuries visible. Scene depicts violence in a non-graphic way, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Exterior of a 7th century Makkah mud-brick home with a small gathering of people, partially lit by moonlight. A slender older Arab man with light olive skin, thin grey-streaked beard with henna tint, off-white thobe and brown cloak, supported by his mother and a Muslim woman, walking towards a doorway. ʿUtbah ibn Rabīʿah shown in the distance, with a rough expression, wearing simple brown thobe and leather sandals. A crowd of people in the background, with some shown from behind. Medium shot, eye level. Soft blue and green palette. No modern objects.
